metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the prompt write (`.rebar/prompts/<id>.md`) and the routing overlay write (`.rebar/criteria_routing.json`) are coordinated atomically — either a single compound writer with rollback on failure, or a defined ordering with compensating cleanup — and confirm `create_prompt`/`update_prompt` are extended (or a new `create_criterion` primitive added) to cover both writes.
findings.finding: The routing-fields authoring write involves two non-atomic file writes — `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md` (via `create_prompt`) AND `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` (the overlay routing entry for the new project criterion) — but the plan treats them as a single operation ('reusing `create_prompt`/`update_prompt`'). A crash or validation failure between the two writes leaves the registry in an inconsistent state: a prompt with no routing entry causes `RegistryError` in `_descriptor_from_prompt` (registry.py:347), or a routing entry with no matching prompt causes the same. The `create_prompt`/`update_prompt` functions in `prompt_library.py` write ONLY the `.md` file — they have no mechanism to touch `criteria_routing.json`.
findings.location: What section / Acceptance Criteria line 1 / Scope: `prompt_library.py`
findings.suggested_fix: Introduce a `create_criterion(cid, prompt_text, routing_fields, *, repo_root)` function that (1) validates routing fields against the overlay schema, (2) writes the prompt .md atomically, (3) reads-and-merges the routing JSON, (4) writes the JSON atomically, and (5) rolls back the .md on JSON write failure. This replaces the plan's implicit 'reuse create_prompt + separately write routing' sequence.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the 'story #3 eval runner' reuse claim with the correct entry point: identify that `passes.pass1_chunk()` (for LLM criteria) and `det_floor.run_det_floor()` (for DET criteria) are the actual preview mechanisms, and specify how the fixture maps to the `PlanContext` / `LLMConfig` / `Runner` inputs each requires.
findings.finding: The plan claims the `/criterion/preview` endpoint reuses 'the story #3 per-criterion eval runner' — but the actual eval runner (`eval_solver.run_case` in `eval_solver.py`) is purpose-built for exactly three prompts (`completion-verifier`, `ticket-quality`, `spec-alignment`) and raises `ValueError` for any other `prompt_id`. Plan-review criterion evaluation goes through `passes.pass1_chunk()` with a `PlanContext`, `LLMConfig`, and `Runner` — a completely different call path that requires a properly constructed context, not a simple fixture dict. The plan's described reuse target does not exist for criteria.
findings.location: What section: 'New POST /criterion/preview endpoint (synchronous)' / Acceptance Criteria line 2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define how `POST /criterion/preview` dispatches between the DET path (`det_floor.run_det_floor`) and the LLM path (`passes.pass1_chunk`), specify which additional fixture fields (ticket_type, children, file_impact) the DET path requires, and document the fixture schema explicitly in the AC.
findings.finding: The preview endpoint AC requires it to work for 'both an LLM criterion and a DET invariant', but the plan gives no dispatch logic for `exec=DET` vs `exec=1-TURN`/`AGENT`. DET invariants (P1-P9) run via `det_floor.run_det_floor(ctx: PlanContext)` — a separate code path from the LLM tier's `pass1_chunk`. The two paths need different inputs (DET needs plan text, ticket_type, children list; LLM needs runner + credentials + LLMConfig), and the preview endpoint's described single contract `{criterion_id | inline(prompt+routing), fixture}` does not specify how `fixture` maps to either path's inputs, nor which path is taken for a given criterion id.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria line 4: 'Authoring + preview work for both an LLM criterion and a DET invariant.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm story #3's per-criterion eval runner is either already landed (cite the callable) or add a hard dependency + spike to define the runner's interface before specifying the preview endpoint contract around it.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that POST /criterion/preview will reuse 'the story #3 per-criterion eval runner' as if it already exists, but no such single-criterion runner function is present in the codebase. ADR 0015 explicitly lists 'per-criterion eval runner' as a deferred follow-on story. The preview endpoint cannot be implemented as described until that runner is built.
findings.location: What section: 'reusing the story #3 per-criterion eval runner behind the agents extra + credentials'; Scope: 'Depends on story #1 … and story #3 (the runner the preview reuses)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the fixture schema for DET invariant preview: specify which PlanContext fields the fixture must supply (at minimum: ticket_type, description/title, state.file_impact, children) and which DET check(s) the preview runs, so AC4's DET path has a concrete integration contract.
findings.finding: AC4 requires 'Authoring + preview work for both an LLM criterion and a DET invariant,' but the DET floor's existing runner (run_det_floor) consumes a PlanContext (ticket_id, title, description, state, children, ticket_type) — a full ticket context — while the plan's proposed preview request shape is {criterion_id | inline(prompt+routing), fixture}. The plan does not specify how a 'fixture' maps to PlanContext fields, nor how the user selects which DET check (P1–P9) to invoke, nor whether the full DET floor (all 9 checks) or a single check is run.
findings.location: AC4: 'Authoring + preview work for both an LLM criterion and a DET invariant'; What section: request shape '{criterion_id | inline(prompt+routing), fixture}'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a DET-specific fixture shape to the request contract, e.g.: `{criterion_id: 'P1'|...|'DET', fixture: {ticket_type, title, description, state?, children?}}` → maps directly to PlanContext, then calls the specific Pn check or run_det_floor.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the DET-criterion dispatch path for `POST /criterion/preview`: define which `PlanContext` fields the fixture must supply, how the `DetResult` maps to the `fire`/`no-fire` verdict, and that this path requires no LLM credentials — separate from the LLM runner reuse claim.
findings.finding: The AC 'authoring + preview work for both an LLM criterion and a DET invariant' is structurally under-specified: the DET tier (`exec=DET`, det_floor.py P1–P9) uses a completely different executor (no LLM runner, returns `DetResult` dataclass) from the LLM tiers. The plan says preview 'reuses the story #3 per-criterion eval runner' but this runner does not cover `exec=DET`, so the dispatch protocol for DET criteria in the preview endpoint is undefined.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — '[ ] Authoring + preview work for both an LLM criterion and a DET invariant.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a description in the plan body (What or Scope) of how the preview endpoint handles DET invariants — specifically which runner path is invoked, whether credentials/agents extra are required, and how the verdict/finding shape differs from the LLM criterion path.
findings.finding: AC4 ('Authoring + preview work for both an LLM criterion and a DET invariant') requires DET invariant execution, but the plan body describes only the LLM eval runner path (story #3, agents extra + credentials). The DET invariant path — which is deterministic, requires no LLM call, and likely uses a different runner branch — is entirely absent from the What/Scope sections, leaving AC4 with no covering implementation description.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4; What section (POST /criterion/preview description)
findings.suggested_fix: In the POST /criterion/preview description, add a branch: 'For DET invariants, invoke the deterministic runner (no LLM call, no credentials required) and return the same {verdict, finding, rationale} shape; for LLM criteria, use the story #3 eval runner with agents extra + credentials.' Update AC4 to reference this branch explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the `verdict` enum vocabulary for `POST /criterion/preview` (e.g. `"fire"|"no-fire"`) AND specify the exact mapping rule from the underlying criterion eval runner's output (`PASS`/`FAIL` / findings-present) to these tokens, so the endpoint contract is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The preview response `verdict` enum vocabulary (`"fire"|"no-fire"`) is never defined or mapped to the existing criterion eval contracts. The codebase uses `PASS`/`FAIL` for completion_verdict and a findings list for review_result — neither matches the plan's invented `fire`/`no-fire` tokens, and no translation rule is specified.
findings.location: What section — `POST /criterion/preview` response contract; AC: '`POST /criterion/preview` runs a live must-fire/must-not-fire preview with the defined request/response contract'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What/AC: '`verdict: "fire"` when the criterion runner returns FAIL (or non-empty findings); `"no-fire"` when it returns PASS (empty findings). For a DET criterion, `"fire"` maps to `status=="fail"` in DetResult.' Alternatively, reuse the existing `completion_verdict` shape and avoid the new vocabulary entirely.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the concrete async fallback API surface: specify the job-creation response shape (e.g. `{job_id, poll_url}`), the poll endpoint path and response schema (`{status: pending|complete|error, result?}`), polling interval recommendation, and job TTL.
findings.finding: The async job+poll fallback is described as something to 'define' but the plan provides no definition — endpoint path, polling interval, job status schema (pending/complete/error fields), and TTL are all left for the executor to design. This is a placeholder masquerading as a decision.
findings.location: What section — 'fall back to an async job+poll shape (define the job id + poll endpoint)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'Async fallback: POST /criterion/preview returns 202 {job_id: str, poll_url: str}; GET /criterion/preview/{job_id} returns {status: "pending"|"complete"|"error", result?: <preview response>, error?: str}; jobs expire after 5 minutes.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the full async job lifecycle: who creates the job entry and when, how concurrent requests are handled (lock/CAS or per-job file), the expiry/eviction policy for completed jobs, and the poll endpoint's behavior for missing/expired job IDs.
findings.finding: The async job+poll fallback introduces shared job state whose full lifecycle (creation ownership, concurrency safety, expiry/retirement) is entirely unspecified.
findings.location: ## What — Spike gate: 'define the job id + poll endpoint'
findings.suggested_fix: Specify: (1) job entries stored in a thread-safe dict protected by a lock or in a temp directory (one file per job-id); (2) TTL-based eviction (e.g., 5 min after completion); (3) poll returns 404 with `{error: 'expired'}` after TTL; (4) POST /criterion/preview is idempotent on the same (criterion_id, fixture) pair within the TTL window.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Decompose into at least two child tickets: (1) a time-boxed spike that resolves sync-vs-async and defines the `POST /criterion/preview` contract + async job/poll fallback, and (2) the authoring form + preview UI gated on the spike outcome — keeping each child executable in one session.
findings.finding: The ticket joins two structurally independent goals ('criterion authoring form' and 'live preview endpoint') in a single leaf ticket with no child decomposition, despite the plan itself calling the preview 'a structurally independent deliverable (its own endpoint + spike).' All four G5 decomposition signals fire: >3 files, ≥3 layers, a new HTTP interface, and a spike gate whose outcome determines the entire async/sync shape. The riskiest piece (sync-vs-async decision + preview endpoint) is not sequenced as a gated prerequisite; instead everything is bundled as one delivery, creating big-bang risk.
findings.location: ## What / ## Scope / Acceptance Criteria (whole ticket)
findings.suggested_fix: Child 1 (spike, ≤1 day): Run a single sync preview call against the reference model; if p95 < 45s declare sync feasible and document the contract; otherwise define the job-id + poll endpoint shape. Output: an ADR or updated plan section. Child 2 (preview endpoint, ~1 day): Implement `POST /criterion/preview` (sync or async per spike outcome) with the eval runner integration. Child 3 (authoring form, ~1 day): Implement the routing-fields form + overlay writing, wiring in the preview UI against the now-stable endpoint.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add non-happy-path handling to the preview UI and routing-fields form: specify how API/auth errors, invalid form fields, malformed fixtures, and missing criterion_id are surfaced to the user (distinct from the timeout/async fallback path already covered).
findings.finding: The plan specifies timeout handling (spike gate, async fallback) but leaves all other non-happy-path preview states unspecified: LLM API auth failures, network errors, malformed fixture input, criterion_id not found, and unexpected LLM response shapes. Similarly, the routing-fields form has no described validation or error-display behavior for invalid field values (e.g., out-of-range block_threshold, missing required fields).
findings.location: What / POST /criterion/preview + editor_assets/ form
findings.suggested_fix: In the Acceptance Criteria or a new 'Error states' subsection, enumerate at minimum: (1) preview API error → show error message with status code; (2) form field validation (block_threshold range, required fields) → inline field errors before submission; (3) malformed fixture → parse error message before calling /criterion/preview; (4) criterion_id not found → 404 message in the preview panel.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit ACs (or a testing-plan section) for the `POST /criterion/preview` failure paths: timeout expiry response shape, async job+poll terminal states (if async fallback is chosen), malformed/unresolvable request error responses, and eval-runner exception handling.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new user-facing `POST /criterion/preview` endpoint with a configurable timeout (default 60s) and an async fallback path, but neither the AC set nor the 'What' section specifies any test coverage for: (a) the timeout path (what does the endpoint return when the eval runner exceeds the configured timeout?), (b) the async job+poll fallback behavior (job-id returned, poll endpoint contract, terminal states), (c) invalid/malformed payloads (missing fixture, unresolvable criterion_id, bad routing fields), or (d) eval-runner error paths (missing credentials, model refusal, runner exception). Only the happy path ('runs a live must-fire/must-not-fire preview') appears in AC #2.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC #2 (`POST /criterion/preview`)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC #2: 'Timeout path: when eval exceeds the configured timeout, the endpoint returns {error: "timeout", job_id?: str} within 1s of the deadline. Async path (if chosen by spike): GET /criterion/preview/{job_id} returns {status: pending|complete|error, result?: ...}. Malformed payload: returns HTTP 422 with a structured error. Credentials absent: returns HTTP 503 with a human-readable message.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the error/degraded response shape for `POST /criterion/preview` (e.g. `{verdict: "error", rationale: str, unavailable: bool}`) to cover LLM unavailability, structured-output parse failure, and pre-fallback timeout, mirroring the pattern used by the existing `/validate` handler.
findings.finding: No fallback response shape is specified for `POST /criterion/preview` when the criterion eval runner itself fails (LLM unavailable, structured-output parse error, or timeout exception before the spike-gate's job+poll fallback applies). The existing completion verifier has an explicit `INDETERMINATE` path; the preview endpoint has none.
findings.location: What section — `POST /criterion/preview`; Spike gate paragraph; AC line 2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a live/end-to-end acceptance criterion for `POST /criterion/preview` that exercises the real eval runner with valid credentials against the reference model (can be a manual smoke-test gate or an integration-test marked `@requires_credentials`), explicitly distinguishing it from the unit-level mock-based tests.
findings.finding: The preview endpoint's core value is executing the eval runner against a live LLM ('reusing the story #3 per-criterion eval runner behind the agents extra + credentials'), but no acceptance criterion requires a live/end-to-end test that actually invokes the real model boundary. AC #4 ('works for both an LLM criterion and a DET invariant') could be satisfied by an offline mock that stubs the eval runner, leaving the live credential + model-call path untested — a proxy-validation risk for a new user-facing flow whose entire purpose is live execution.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC #4; ## What — preview endpoint description
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: 'A live integration test (skipped when `REBAR_TEST_CREDENTIALS` is absent) posts a known must-fire fixture to `POST /criterion/preview` and asserts `verdict == "fire"` using the real eval runner — not a stub — confirming the end-to-end path from HTTP handler through eval runner to model response.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the `applies_at` sub-schema (field name — not `applies_to` — plus the value vocabulary for `levels`, `suppress_when`, and `suppress_types`) in the plan's routing-fields form definition, or reference the exact registry schema as the authoritative spec.
findings.finding: The routing-fields form references `applies_at/applies_to` but never defines the sub-schema. In the codebase `applies_at` is a struct `{levels: [...], container_only: bool, suppress_types: [...], suppress_when: [...]}` — the plan provides no vocabulary for any of these sub-fields, and uses `applies_to` as an apparent alias for `applies_at` which does not exist in the registry.
findings.location: What section — 'routing-fields form (exec, applies_at/applies_to, block_threshold, default_posture/blocking_enabled, fail_mode)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `applies_at/applies_to` in the plan with `applies_at` only, and add: 'sub-fields: `levels` (subset of `["epic","story","task"]`), `container_only` (bool), `suppress_when` (values: `"test_task"`,`"mechanical_leaf"`), `suppress_types` (e.g. `["bug"]`).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either remove `fail_mode` from the routing-fields form specification (it is not a real routing key) or define it as a new schema key with its value vocabulary, validation rule, and semantic effect in the registry.
findings.finding: `fail_mode` is listed as a routing-fields form field but this key does not exist in the criteria routing schema (`criteria_routing.json`), `_validate_routing_entry`, or any registry descriptor. It is a phantom field that will be silently ignored or cause a validation error.
findings.location: What section — 'routing-fields form (exec, applies_at/applies_to, block_threshold, default_posture/blocking_enabled, fail_mode)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or explicit done-definition within AC1/AC2) specifying that the routing-fields form and preview output meet WCAG 2.1 AA — at minimum: all form controls keyboard-operable (SC 2.1.1), verdict/finding conveyed by text not color alone (SC 1.4.1), and form fields labeled for screen readers (SC 1.3.1).
findings.finding: The plan introduces new user-facing UI (routing-fields form + preview UI in editor_assets/) with no accessibility specification — no mention of WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, keyboard navigation, screen-reader support, color contrast, reduced motion, or touch-target sizing.
findings.location: Scope — 'editor_assets/ (form + preview UI)'; What — 'routing-fields form'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the sequencing contradiction: either promote the spike gate to a separate prerequisite story/task that must close before this story's UI work begins, or demote it to an in-story first-step with a clear definition of done before the UI subtask starts.
findings.finding: The spike gate is framed as a pre-condition in the 'What' section ('resolve before committing UI') but appears as a co-deliverable in the Acceptance Criteria (AC3), creating a sequencing contradiction: an agent cannot simultaneously treat it as a prerequisite and as a same-story deliverable.
findings.location: What section ('Spike gate: resolve before committing UI') vs. Acceptance Criteria AC3

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define AC4's pass criterion concretely: specify a named fixture (or fixture schema), the invocation command (e.g. `pytest tests/test_criterion_preview.py -k det_invariant`), and the expected observable outcome (verdict + finding shape) for both criterion types.
findings.finding: AC4 states 'Authoring + preview work for both an LLM criterion and a DET invariant' but 'work' is undefined — no test fixture, no invocation command, and no pass criterion (e.g. correct verdict returned, file written to expected path) is specified, leaving the acceptance bar entirely to the executor's judgment.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Make AC3 unconditionally verifiable by specifying the artifact that proves the spike gate was resolved in both branches: e.g. a documented benchmark result against the reference model AND either a passing sync integration test or an implemented async poll endpoint.
findings.finding: AC3 ('the spike gate is resolved') is conditionally observable: if the sync path succeeds within 60s, no async artifact is produced, and the AC can be trivially 'passed' with only a decision note. The AC does not specify what durable, verifiable artifact constitutes 'resolved' in the happy-path case.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'The spike gate is resolved: a defined pass/fail threshold and a concrete async job+poll fallback if sync execution is infeasible'
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite AC3 as: 'A benchmark result (fixture + model + latency) is committed proving sync completes within 60s OR the async job+poll endpoints are implemented and tested; the chosen path is recorded in a decision note in the PR.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a requirement to record the spike resolution (sync vs. async choice, rationale, and job-store design if async) in an ADR or CLAUDE.md entry before the UI is committed.
findings.finding: The novel architectural decision to introduce a live-preview endpoint with a sync/async fallback into the editor's ThreadingHTTPServer has no planned ADR, CLAUDE.md entry, or design-doc update.
findings.location: ## What — Spike gate / POST /criterion/preview
findings.suggested_fix: After the spike gate resolves, write a short ADR (or CLAUDE.md section) covering: (1) the chosen sync/async shape, (2) measured latency vs. threshold, (3) job-store design if async, (4) why ThreadingHTTPServer was kept rather than replaced.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the abandon/cancel exit path for the criterion authoring flow: specify dirty-state detection, a discard confirmation, or equivalent guard so users can exit without unintended writes to the .rebar/ overlay.
findings.finding: The authoring flow's exit points are underspecified: only the success path (criterion written to .rebar/ overlay + activation) is described. There is no mention of what happens when a user abandons mid-authoring (partial form state, unsaved changes), nor whether a cancel/discard action is available.
findings.location: What / editor_assets/ form + Acceptance Criteria

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that overlay file writes are atomic (write to a temp file then `os.replace`) and document whether the editor is the sole writer or whether CLI tooling can concurrently mutate the same overlay files.
findings.finding: The `.rebar/` overlay files written by the routing-fields form are shared mutable state with no defined concurrency or atomicity guarantee.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria — 'writing the .rebar/ overlay + activate list'
findings.suggested_fix: Use `write to .rebar/<criterion>.yaml.tmp` followed by `os.replace(tmp, target)` to guarantee atomic writes on POSIX. Add a note in the scope section clarifying that the editor holds exclusive write ownership during a session, or add an advisory lock if CLI co-authoring is expected.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
